pullRequest.body: Refactor event names to follow domain-driven naming convention (e.g. 'orderCompleted' -> 'order.finalized')

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling CheckoutService.completeOrder to assess downstream impact
Find services calling PaymentService.capturePayment to assess downstream impact
Find services calling OnboardingService.registerUser to assess downstream impact
Find services calling WarehouseService.updateStock to assess downstream impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The event name was renamed from 'orderCompleted' to 'order.finalized' on line 28, but CheckoutService.completeOrder() in src/services/checkout.service.ts still emits 'orderCompleted'. This event will silently go unhandled -- warehouse notification and analytics tracking will stop firing for completed orders.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from 'orderCompleted' -- update CheckoutService emitter
this.register('order.finalized', this.handleOrderComplete.bind(this));
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed event 'orderCompleted' to 'order.finalized' breaks CheckoutService which still emits the old name
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The event name was renamed from 'paymentProcessed' to 'payment.captured' on line 29, but PaymentService.capturePayment() in src/services/payment.service.ts still emits 'paymentProcessed'. The payment handler will never fire, so revenue recording silently stops working.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from 'paymentProcessed' -- update PaymentService emitter
this.register('payment.captured', this.handlePayment.bind(this));
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed event 'paymentProcessed' to 'payment.captured' breaks PaymentService which still emits the old name
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The event name was renamed from 'userRegistered' to 'user.onboarded' on line 30, but OnboardingService.registerUser() in src/services/onboarding.service.ts still emits 'userRegistered'. The welcome email handler will never fire for new users.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from 'userRegistered' -- update OnboardingService emitter
this.register('user.onboarded', this.handleUserRegistration.bind(this));
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed event 'userRegistered' to 'user.onboarded' breaks OnboardingService which still emits the old name
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The event name was renamed from 'inventoryUpdated' to 'inventory.adjusted' on line 31, but WarehouseService.updateStock() in src/services/warehouse.service.ts still emits 'inventoryUpdated'. The reorder-threshold check will never fire, risking stock-outs.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from 'inventoryUpdated' -- update WarehouseService emitter
this.register('inventory.adjusted', this.handleInventory.bind(this));
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed event 'inventoryUpdated' to 'inventory.adjusted' breaks WarehouseService which still emits the old name
codeSuggestions.label: bug

pullRequest.body: Restructure user query return types for the new permissions model and rename fields for consistency

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find middleware using authenticateRequest to verify request pipeline impact
Find controllers calling ProfileController.getProfile to verify API layer impact
Find services calling TeamService.hasCapacity to assess downstream impact
Find upstream dependency Permission to verify interface contract and type definitions
Find upstream dependency Database to verify interface contract and type definitions

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The return type of findUserWithAccess() changed from { user, roles: string[] } to { user, permissions: Permission[] } (lines 19-34), but authenticateRequest() in src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts still accesses result.roles. Since result.roles is now undefined: (1) req.userRoles is set to undefined, and (2) the admin check result.roles?.includes('admin') always returns undefined (falsy), blocking ALL users -- including admins -- from /admin routes.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed return field 'roles' to 'permissions' breaks auth middleware -- all admin access silently blocked
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The findById() method now returns displayName instead of fullName (line 15), but ProfileController.getProfile() in src/controllers/profile.controller.ts still accesses user.fullName. This field is now undefined, so the API response will return name: undefined for all user profiles.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed return field 'fullName' to 'displayName' breaks ProfileController which still reads user.fullName
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The getTeamMembers() method now returns totalMembers instead of memberCount (line 43), but TeamService.hasCapacity() in src/services/team.service.ts still reads team.memberCount. Since memberCount is undefined, the comparison undefined < MAX_TEAM_SIZE evaluates to false, so hasCapacity() always returns false and no new members can ever be added to any team.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed return field 'memberCount' to 'totalMembers' breaks TeamService capacity check -- blocks all new members
codeSuggestions.label: bug

pullRequest.body: Restructure application config into nested namespaces for better organization (CACHE_TTL -> config.cache.ttl, etc.)

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find consumers of CacheManager.constructor to verify impact of changes in app-config
Find services calling HttpClient.requestWithRetry to assess downstream impact
Find consumers of createPool to verify impact of changes in app-config
Find middleware using createSessionMiddleware to verify request pipeline impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The named export CACHE_TTL was removed and restructured into config.cache.ttl (lines 9-11), but CacheManager in src/infrastructure/cache.ts still imports CACHE_TTL as a named export. This will cause a compile-time error or, in plain JS, CACHE_TTL will be undefined, causing Redis setex to use undefined as TTL and default to no expiration.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed CACHE_TTL named export breaks CacheManager which still imports the old constant
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The named export MAX_RETRIES was removed and restructured into config.http.maxRetries (lines 12-14), but HttpClient in src/services/http-client.ts still imports MAX_RETRIES. The default parameter retries: number = MAX_RETRIES will be undefined, causing the retry loop condition attempt <= undefined to always be false, meaning zero retries on failure.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed MAX_RETRIES named export breaks HttpClient which uses it as default retry count
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The named export DB_POOL_SIZE was removed and restructured into config.database.poolSize (lines 15-17), but createPool() in src/infrastructure/database.ts still imports DB_POOL_SIZE. The pool will be created with max: undefined, which pg may interpret as unlimited connections, potentially exhausting the database connection limit.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed DB_POOL_SIZE named export breaks database.ts pool creation -- may allow unlimited connections
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The named export SESSION_TIMEOUT was removed and restructured into config.auth.sessionTimeout (lines 18-20), but createSessionMiddleware() in src/middleware/session.ts still imports SESSION_TIMEOUT. The cookie maxAge will be SESSION_TIMEOUT * 1000 = NaN (undefined * 1000), causing the session cookie to have no valid expiration. Depending on the express-session implementation, this may create sessions that never expire or expire immediately.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed SESSION_TIMEOUT named export breaks session middleware -- cookie maxAge becomes NaN
codeSuggestions.label: bug

pullRequest.body: Refactor validation utilities to use options objects instead of positional parameters for extensibility

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find controllers calling AuthController.register to verify API layer impact
Find middleware using sanitizeBody to verify request pipeline impact
Find services calling ImportService.processRow to assess downstream impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The validateEmail() signature was changed from (email: string, allowPlus: boolean) to (opts: ValidateEmailOptions) on line 21, but AuthController in src/controllers/auth.controller.ts still calls validateEmail(email, true) and validateEmail(email, false) with positional arguments. The email string will be treated as the opts object, and opts.email will be undefined, so regex.test(undefined) returns false -- all registration and login attempts are rejected with 'Invalid email'.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Signature changed from (email, allowPlus) to (opts) -- update AuthController
export function validateEmail(opts: ValidateEmailOptions): boolean {
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed validateEmail from positional args to options object breaks AuthController -- all emails rejected
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The sanitizeInput() signature was changed from (input: string, maxLength: number) to (opts: SanitizeInputOptions) on line 27, but the sanitizeBody and sanitizeQueryParams middleware in src/middleware/input-sanitizer.ts still call sanitizeInput(content, 5000), sanitizeInput(title, 200), and sanitizeInput(value, 500) with positional arguments. The string will be passed as opts, and opts.input will be undefined, so the function will attempt .replace() on undefined and throw a TypeError.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Signature changed from (input, maxLength) to (opts) -- update input-sanitizer middleware
export function sanitizeInput(opts: SanitizeInputOptions): string {
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed sanitizeInput from positional args to options object breaks input-sanitizer middleware -- TypeError on every request
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The parseDate() signature was changed from (dateStr: string, format: string) to (opts: ParseDateOptions) on line 36, but ImportService.processRow() in src/services/import.service.ts still calls parseDate(row.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD') and parseDate(row.updatedAt, 'ISO') with positional arguments. The date string is passed as opts, opts.input is undefined, and new Date(undefined) returns Invalid Date -- all imported rows will have invalid timestamps.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Signature changed from (dateStr, format) to (opts) -- update ImportService
export function parseDate(opts: ParseDateOptions): Date {
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed parseDate from positional args to options object breaks ImportService -- all imported dates are Invalid Date
codeSuggestions.label: bug

inputs.patchWithLinesStr:
## file: 'src/types/order-status.ts'
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
__new hunk__
1 +export enum OrderStatus {
2 +  PENDING = 'pending',
3 +  IN_PROGRESS = 'in_progress',
4 +  DISPATCHED = 'dispatched',
5 +  DELIVERED = 'delivered',
6 +  VOIDED = 'voided',
7 +}
__old hunk__
1 -export enum OrderStatus {
2 -  PENDING_PAYMENT = 'pending_payment',
3 -  PROCESSING = 'processing',
4 -  SHIPPED = 'shipped',
5 -  DELIVERED = 'delivered',
6 -  CANCELLED = 'cancelled',
7 -  REFUND_REQUESTED = 'refund_requested',
8 -}

pullRequest.body: Simplify order status workflow: merge intermediate statuses (PENDING_PAYMENT->PENDING, PROCESSING->IN_PROGRESS, SHIPPED->DISPATCHED, CANCELLED->VOIDED, remove REFUND_REQUESTED)

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find services calling OrderService.handleNewOrder to assess downstream impact
Find services calling FulfillmentService.processOrders to assess downstream impact
Find services calling ShippingService.getTrackingInfo to assess downstream impact
Find services calling AnalyticsService.getCancellationRate to assess downstream impact
Find services calling RefundService.processRefund to assess downstream impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The enum value PENDING_PAYMENT was removed and merged into PENDING (line 2), but OrderService.handleNewOrder() in src/services/order.service.ts has a switch case on OrderStatus.PENDING_PAYMENT. Since this enum member no longer exists, TypeScript will report a compile error, and in plain JS the switch case will never match, so new orders in pending-payment state fall through to the default case and throw an unexpected error.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed PENDING_PAYMENT enum value breaks OrderService switch case -- new orders in that state throw error
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The enum value PROCESSING was renamed to IN_PROGRESS (line 3), but FulfillmentService.processOrders() in src/services/fulfillment.service.ts still references OrderStatus.PROCESSING to query and OrderStatus.SHIPPED to update. Both are undefined, so findByStatus receives undefined and returns no orders -- the entire fulfillment pipeline silently stops processing.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from PROCESSING -- update FulfillmentService
  IN_PROGRESS = 'in_progress',
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed PROCESSING to IN_PROGRESS breaks FulfillmentService -- fulfillment pipeline stops processing orders
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The enum value SHIPPED was renamed to DISPATCHED (line 4), but ShippingService in src/services/shipping.service.ts still checks OrderStatus.SHIPPED in getTrackingInfo() and confirmDelivery(). Since SHIPPED is undefined, the comparison order.status !== undefined is always true, so getTrackingInfo() returns null for all shipped orders and confirmDelivery() always throws 'Can only confirm delivery for shipped orders'.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from SHIPPED -- update ShippingService
  DISPATCHED = 'dispatched',
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed SHIPPED to DISPATCHED breaks ShippingService -- tracking info unavailable and delivery cannot be confirmed
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The enum value CANCELLED was renamed to VOIDED (line 6), but AnalyticsService in src/services/analytics.service.ts still references OrderStatus.CANCELLED, OrderStatus.PROCESSING, and OrderStatus.SHIPPED in getCancellationRate() and getDashboardMetrics(). All three are undefined, so the cancellation rate query and dashboard metrics return zero/incorrect values, silently corrupting all analytics reporting.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
// WARNING: Renamed from CANCELLED -- update AnalyticsService
  VOIDED = 'voided',
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed CANCELLED to VOIDED breaks AnalyticsService -- cancellation rate and dashboard metrics return wrong values
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The enum value REFUND_REQUESTED was removed and merged into VOIDED (line 6), but RefundService in src/services/refund.service.ts still references OrderStatus.REFUND_REQUESTED in processRefund() and getRefundQueue(). Since the enum member no longer exists, the refund status check fails at compile time (or compares against undefined at runtime), so no refunds can ever be processed and the refund queue always returns empty.
codeSuggestions.existingCode:
export enum OrderStatus {
  PENDING = 'pending',
  IN_PROGRESS = 'in_progress',
  DISPATCHED = 'dispatched',
  DELIVERED = 'delivered',
  VOIDED = 'voided',
}
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed REFUND_REQUESTED enum value breaks RefundService -- refund processing and queue both fail
codeSuggestions.label: bug
